metadata.title: Rumor Tracker & Fact Check
metadata.description: Debunking misinformation and verifying trending claims about the Iran-Israel conflict. Each entry is independently researched with linked sources.
metadata.methodology: Claims are sourced from trending social media posts, news outlets, and government statements. Each is independently verified against multiple sources. Verdicts: Confirmed (verified true), Debunked (verified false), Likely (evidence supports but not fully confirmed), Unlikely (evidence contradicts but not fully debunked), Unverified (insufficient evidence either way).

claims.claim: Iran has surrendered to Israel and the US
claims.summary: Viral Facebook and TikTok posts on March 4 claimed Iran surrendered after Tehran was bombarded. In reality, Iranian President Pezeshkian said those dreaming of unconditional surrender 'will take that dream to their graves.' Iran did apologize to neighboring countries for collateral strikes but explicitly rejected any notion of surrender.

sources.name: Tempo Fact Check — Claim Iran surrendered is false
Iran International — Iran will never surrender unconditionally
PBS — Iran shows no signs of surrender

claims.origin: Viral Facebook and TikTok posts

claims.claim: Iran sank the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier
claims.summary: Posts went viral (one by Kenyan MP Peter Salasya reached 6M+ views) claiming Iran sank the carrier. PolitiFact found no credible reports supporting the claim, and the vessel markings in the circulated video did not match real photos of the Lincoln. CENTCOM confirmed the ship was not hit.

sources.name: PolitiFact — Social media awash with Iran war misinformation
Poynter — Old and AI-generated videos fueling misinformation

claims.origin: Viral X/Twitter post by Kenyan MP Peter Salasya (6M+ views)

claims.claim: Iran captured 173 US Delta Force soldiers — images show them in custody
claims.summary: Ali Larijani (Iran's National Security Council head) claimed on X that Iran captured US soldiers. A separate viral post claimed 173 Delta Force soldiers were captured, using AI-generated images — one bearing a visible Google Gemini AI watermark. CENTCOM called the claims 'lies and deceptions.'

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Trump admin denies Iran captured US soldiers
Yahoo News / Lead Stories — 173 Delta Force NOT captured, photos are AI
Jerusalem Post — CENTCOM denies Iran captured US soldiers

claims.origin: Ali Larijani on X, IRGC-affiliated Telegram channels, AI-generated images with Google Gemini watermark

claims.claim: Israel bombed North Korea's embassy in Tehran; Kim Jong Un called it a 'big mistake'
claims.summary: A viral claim circulated that Israel deliberately struck the North Korean embassy and that Kim Jong Un issued a threatening statement. Snopes found no evidence the embassy was targeted, and the alleged Kim Jong Un quote was fabricated. A strike map showed a hit near (but not at) the embassy.

sources.name: Snopes — Did Israel bomb North Korea's embassy in Iran?
Yahoo News — Fact Check on North Korea embassy claim

claims.origin: Viral social media posts across X/Twitter and Facebook

claims.claim: Iran was days/weeks away from a nuclear weapon — justifying the strikes
claims.summary: Trump and others claimed Iran was on the brink of having a nuclear weapon. IAEA Director General Grossi said on March 2: 'We don't see a structured program to manufacture nuclear weapons.' Arms control experts confirmed there was no evidence of imminent weaponization, though Iran's enriched uranium stockpile had shortened potential timelines.

sources.name: FactCheck.org — Assessing Trump's claims on Iran's nuclear capabilities
Scientific American — Iran was nowhere close to a nuclear bomb
Arms Control Association — Did Iran pose an imminent threat? No.

claims.origin: Trump administration statements, US government officials

claims.claim: Iran hit 4 ballistic missiles on USS Abraham Lincoln
claims.summary: The semi-official Iranian outlet Mehr reported that four Iranian ballistic missiles hit the USS Abraham Lincoln. CENTCOM clarified the Lincoln was not hit and the missiles did not even come close. NewsGuard flagged this as one of 18 false war-related claims by Iran.

sources.name: CNN — Fake, AI-generated images and videos of the Iran war
Euronews — Iran's state media ramps up disinformation

claims.origin: Iran's Mehr News Agency (semi-official)

claims.claim: 650 US troops killed or wounded in first two days of the war
claims.summary: An IRGC spokesperson claimed 650 US troops were killed or wounded, published by military-aligned Tasnim. CENTCOM rebutted this, stating six US service members had been killed at that point. As of March 13, the confirmed US death toll is 13 (including 6 from the KC-135 crash), with ~140 wounded.

sources.name: Time — What we know about US service members killed
Erkan's Field Diary — Disinformation in the Iran-Israel-US war

claims.origin: IRGC spokesperson via Tasnim News Agency

claims.claim: AI-generated images show Khamenei's body found under rubble
claims.summary: After the February 28 strikes killed Supreme Leader Khamenei, images purporting to show his body being recovered from rubble went viral. These were confirmed to be AI-generated fabrications by multiple fact-checking organizations.

sources.name: Poynter — AI-generated videos fueling misinformation
CNN — Fake, AI-generated images and videos of the Iran war

claims.origin: AI-generated content shared on X/Twitter and Telegram

claims.claim: Video shows Iranian missiles hitting Tel Aviv — massive explosion
claims.summary: A video posted on X captioned 'THIS IS TEL AVIV. THANK YOU IRAN!' actually showed the 2015 Tianjin, China chemical warehouse explosion. Separate fake videos showing 'missile barrages hitting Tel Aviv' were traced to Algeria and to the video game Arma 3.

sources.name: Euronews — Did you spot these fake videos about the Iran war?
Poynter — Old and AI-generated videos fueling misinformation

claims.origin: Recycled 2015 Tianjin explosion footage and Arma 3 video game clips

claims.claim: US KC-135 Stratotanker crash killed all 6 crew members on board
claims.summary: CENTCOM confirmed the loss of a KC-135 Stratotanker refueling aircraft near Turaibil in western Iraq on March 12-13. All 6 crew members were confirmed killed. This is the 4th manned US aircraft lost since Operation Epic Fury began. A second KC-135 landed at Ben Gurion Airport with tail fin damage — possible mid-air collision under investigation.

sources.name: CNN — US Air Force KC-135 lost over Iraq
Al Jazeera — 6 US service members killed
CNBC — KC-135 crash details

claims.origin: CENTCOM official statement and multiple verified news outlets

claims.claim: Coordinated IRGC-linked fake account networks are spreading propaganda on social media
claims.summary: A Clemson University report identified at least 62 accounts across X, Bluesky, and Instagram linked to Iran's IRGC, posing as users from the Americas, UK, Scotland, and Ireland. X identified a single person in Pakistan managing 31 hacked accounts renamed to 'Iran War Monitor' variants. The account 'Daily Iran Military' surged from 700K to 1.4M followers in under a week.

sources.name: Times of Israel — X cracks down on AI-generated war footage
Fox News — Iranian regime spreading propaganda across dozens of accounts

claims.origin: Clemson University research, X platform investigations

claims.claim: Iran's internet is at 4% capacity — government hiding massive casualties
claims.summary: Iran's internet connectivity has dropped to approximately 4% of normal capacity according to NetBlocks and Cloudflare Radar. This makes independent verification of casualty figures nearly impossible. Hengaw Organization reports 4,300+ killed — triple the government's official figure. The near-total blackout is consistent with deliberate government censorship during the conflict.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran death toll tracker
Hengaw Organization — At least 4,300 killed in first 10 days

claims.origin: NetBlocks internet monitoring, Cloudflare Radar, human rights organizations

claims.claim: Iran's official death toll (~1,444) vastly undercounts actual casualties
claims.summary: Iran's Health Ministry reports ~1,444 killed. However, the Hengaw Organization estimates 4,300+ killed in the first 10 days alone (including 3,910 military). The gap exists because Iran's official figures appear to exclude most military casualties. Neither figure can be independently verified due to Iran's internet blackout (4% capacity).

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran says 1,255 killed, mostly civilians
Hengaw — At least 4,300 killed including 390 civilians
Modern Diplomacy — Counting the casualties

claims.origin: Discrepancy between Iranian Health Ministry and independent human rights organizations

claims.claim: Iran's strikes have had 'no impact' on its military capabilities
claims.summary: Iranian officials claimed US-Israeli strikes caused no meaningful damage to military capabilities. Euronews investigated and found satellite imagery and independent analysis contradicting this, showing substantial damage to nuclear sites (Natanz, Fordow), military facilities, and air defense systems. CENTCOM confirmed 5,000+ targets struck.

sources.name: Euronews — Are Iran's claims that strikes had 'no impact' true?

claims.origin: Iranian government official statements

claims.claim: A French soldier was killed in Iraq — first non-US NATO casualty
claims.summary: The French Ministry of Armed Forces confirmed that Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion of the 7th Alpine Chasseurs Battalion was killed in a drone strike near Makhmour in the Erbil region of Iraq on March 12. Five additional French soldiers were wounded. This is the first non-US NATO fatality in the conflict. President Macron called the attack 'unacceptable.'

sources.name: BNO News — French soldier killed in Iraq
France 24 — Middle East war live updates

claims.origin: French Ministry of Armed Forces official statement

claims.claim: Trump's claim that there's 'practically nothing left to target' in Iran
claims.summary: CNN compiled Trump's false and contradictory statements about the war, including the claim that there was 'practically nothing left to target.' Strikes continued to intensify after this statement, and CENTCOM continued identifying new targets. The claim contradicts the ongoing operational tempo.

sources.name: CNN — Fact check: Trump's latest false claims about the Iran war

claims.origin: Trump press conference and social media statements

claims.claim: UN Security Council labeled Trump a 'war criminal' after Iran strikes
claims.summary: Viral posts claimed the UN Security Council declared Trump an 'international war criminal' after the Feb. 28 strikes. Snopes rated this FALSE. While the Security Council held an emergency meeting, it issued no resolutions or statements labeling Trump a war criminal. The US is a permanent member with veto power, making such a declaration procedurally impossible. Only Iran's ambassador used the term 'war crime' in reference to the attacks.

sources.name: Snopes — Did UN label Trump a 'war criminal'?
Yahoo News — Fact Check on UN war criminal claim

claims.origin: Viral Facebook, X/Twitter, and Instagram posts

claims.claim: Trump claimed Iran has Tomahawk missiles to deflect blame for a girls' school strike
claims.summary: When asked about a Feb. 28 strike on an Iranian girls' school that killed 175 people (many children), Trump suggested Iran could have fired the Tomahawk cruise missile that hit it. PolitiFact and weapons experts confirmed Iran has no Tomahawks — only the US, UK, Japan, Australia, and Netherlands operate them. CSIS analyst Mark Cancian and Middlebury Institute's Jeffrey Lewis both confirmed Iranian cruise missiles are visually distinct from Tomahawks.

sources.name: PolitiFact — Trump blamed Iran for a strike on a girls' school
PBS — Fact-checking Trump's Tomahawk claim

claims.origin: Trump press conference on Day 10 of the war

claims.claim: Energy Secretary Chris Wright's deleted post about Hormuz tanker escort caused oil to crash 15%
claims.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright posted on X that the US Navy had 'successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz.' The post was deleted within 30 minutes, but not before WTI crude crashed from ~$120 to $77/barrel — a 15% drop. The White House confirmed the claim was false. Reuters reported the Navy had actually refused escort requests since the war began. Wright took 'full ownership' calling it a 'miscommunication.' Multiple analysts and Sen. Chris Murphy accused the administration of market manipulation.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — White House denies Hormuz tanker escort
Common Dreams — Trump energy chief accused of oil market manipulation
Bloomberg — Wright takes responsibility for erroneous post

claims.origin: Chris Wright's deleted X post on March 10, 2026

claims.claim: War Thunder video game footage shared as real Iran war combat — Texas Governor shared it
claims.summary: A War Thunder video game clip showing a WWII-era USS Tennessee shooting down a Messerschmitt jet was shared on X as footage of the Iran war, reaching 7.3 million views. Texas Governor Greg Abbott shared it captioned 'Bye bye' before deleting. AFP and Gaijin Entertainment (War Thunder's developer) confirmed it was game footage. The White House X account also posted Call of Duty clips mixed with real strike footage. Arma 3 gameplay was separately circulated as Iranian missile attacks.

sources.name: PC Gamer — War Thunder clip shared as Iran war footage, 7M views
Euronews — Fake videos about the Iran war

claims.origin: Video game clips posted on X/Twitter as real combat footage

claims.claim: Polymarket insider trading — new accounts made $1.2M betting on Iran strike timing
claims.summary: Six new Polymarket accounts made well-timed bets that the Iran strike would happen on Feb. 28, winning $1.2M total. Most wallets were funded within 24 hours of the attack. One account ('Magamyman') made $553K. Snopes confirmed the suspicious trading patterns but could not verify insider knowledge. Trump Jr. is a Polymarket adviser and his firm invested millions in the platform. The Trump administration dropped two federal investigations into Polymarket. $529M was traded on Iran-related contracts total.

sources.name: Snopes — Polymarket bets on Iran strikes and insider knowledge
NPR — 'Magamyman' made $553K on death of Iran's supreme leader
CNN — Iran prediction market bets scrutinized

claims.origin: Polymarket blockchain transaction analysis, Snopes investigation

claims.claim: White House press secretary did not rule out a military draft when asked directly
claims.summary: Fox Business host Maria Bartiromo asked White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt whether the US would institute a military draft. Leavitt did not rule it out, instead saying 'the president is going to do what's necessary to protect the homeland.' Snopes confirmed she did not explicitly endorse a draft but notably declined to deny one. Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the non-answer. A draft would require Congress to amend the Military Selective Service Act.

sources.name: Snopes — Did White House refuse to rule out a military draft?

claims.origin: Fox Business interview with Karoline Leavitt, March 12, 2026
